title: Fermented Lamb Stew
description: A hearty stew made with tender lamb, rich broth, and a tangy twist from fermented ingredients. Serve with some crusty bread for a satisfying meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb shoulder and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the fermented cabbage and cook for another 2 minutes.
Pour in the Syrian broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with crisp lettuce.
Serve with crusty bread on the side.
